title:
Viewed Changing to a data field isn’t easy, and here's why:

The first weeks in data analytics can feel overwhelming with so much to learn and so many paths you can go with.

Spreadsheets, SQL, BI, coding, AI ... you name it.

This creates a sense of uncertainty that will activate your impostor syndrome, making you feel like every lesson you watch doesn't actually make you more skilled.

The uncertainty gets even heavier when you’re not sure when things will start to make sense.

And, when you find yourself during extended periods in a situation where you feel like you're not improving, chances are you might quit because ... life's too short to waste time on something you cannot understand.

But let me tell you ...

That uncertainty fades.

One Formula at a time.

One KPI at a time.

One Query at a time.

This is the message I have for today:

If you’re just starting out or feeling stuck, keep going.

Progress comes quietly, but it comes.

title:
Viewed Dubai has been a blur. For the past six months, I've had no breaks and no time off work. Wore the same outfit every day, didn’t prep for shoots. 
My days were just about sleeping late, waking up late, partying hard, and catching up with old friends. 
The only thing I stayed consistent with was prayer and meditation. God should always remain the top priority!

title:
Viewed 🌸 Hello DataFans, I've been a little quiet here...I'm deep into Google's Conversational Agents framework for hybrid deterministic/agentic workflows at work. It's a useful visual flow builder where training phrases with intent/entity detection can combine with GenAI instructions and responses to build customer service bots. It would actually make for a pretty interesting Streamlit NLP demo 🤔 

-

Being focused on backend agent work is why I wasn't able to play with Python frontends like Streamlit or Reflex. I also filmed a video about Streamlit and its alternatives 2 weeks ago but I still didn't start editing it 🥲

Because this month of June was packed with travels, from social media photography for an internal tech event, to badminton competitions out of the city to end the season. 

I'm hoping to get back to the video in July, that makes for a 2 month YT break, the Youtube algorithm is probably not going to like it...

-

I'll keep sharing Streamlit news through posts, we're eagerly waiting for the next release which may, or may not, flex boxes eheh

🙃 any fun tech you've been trying out lately?

See you soon DataFans

Viewed 🌸 Hello DataFans, I've been a little quiet here...I'm deep into Google's Conversational Agents framework for hybrid deterministic/agentic workflows at work. It would actually make for a pretty interesting Streamlit NLP demo 🤔 

-

Being focused on backend agent work is why I wasn't able to play with Python frontends like Streamlit or Reflex. I also filmed a video about Streamlit and its alternatives 2 weeks ago but I still didn't start editing it 🥲

Because this month of June was packed with travels, from social media photography for an internal tech event, to badminton competitions out of the city to end the season. 

I'm hoping to get back to the video in July, that makes for a 2 month YT break, the Youtube algorithm is probably not going to like it...

-

I'll keep sharing Streamlit news through posts, we're eagerly waiting for the next release which may, or may not, flex boxes eheh

🙃 any fun tech you've been trying out lately?

See you soon DataFans

title:
Viewed I wrote a book that was born out of questions that used to keep me up at night. 

Now, it’s sold over 1 million copies worldwide??! 

That’s something I never thought I’d write.

I wrote "The Diary of a CEO: The 33 Laws of Business, Marketing and Life" two years ago and I had no idea what would come from it - to make it even more surreal, the book has been translated in over 35 languages since we released it!!

As corny as it sounds, this is one of the dreams that I never allowed myself to dream - not least because of the patience it takes to write a book - but because my spelling is awful, I got crap grades in English and I was told that my writing “was going to hold me back” when I was younger. And here we are...with a book... and no ghostwriters!!!

This book succeeded because of 30~ people behind the scenes that worked overtime with me over the space of 2 years - I was just one part in a very complex smart team. 

This book questions the questions. It’s an amalgamation of wisdom drawn from podcast conversations, scientific principles, and my own experiences.

It distills 33 laws that will guide you not only in your business ventures but also in your personal life.
I’m genuinely so proud of this book & wish it had been available when I was starting out in my career.

Several years ago before I wrote my first book, someone told me that “Books are dead" and that "nobody reads books anymore.” For years, people have eagerly anticipated the death of the book publishing industry.

Yet books have not only survived, they’ve become even more popular in all their forms. If there is one medium of communication that has the greatest chance of becoming part of the counter-movement to a world crowded with AI, Apps and algorithms… It might just be the 600 year old book publishing industry.

To mark one million copies sold and we’re releasing a paperback version with a new cover (it’s black 👀) that is lighter and smaller - perfect for travelling ✈️

You can pre-order it here now: https://smarturl.it/DOACbook

title:
Viewed MGD1 becomes the FIDE World Rapid Team Champion 2025!

On paper, MGD1 was not the strongest side in the tournament! There were quite a few teams that had a higher rating average than them. But what was it that made the team go back home with the gold medal?

Well, it was the camaraderie, team spirit and bonding that all the players shared with each other. 

1. Arjun Erigaisi is an absolute class act and on board no.1 he could beat just about anyone!

2. Harikrishna and Anton Guijarro provided the team with a lot of stability. Harikrishna is an absolute team player and has won so many team tournaments from Bundesliga, to the leagues of various countries, to the Chess Olympiad in 2024 and more! Anton Guijarro played each and every game for the team and scored a very important win against Duda that helped MGD1 beat WR Chess with a 4-2 score.

3. Pranav V. and Leon Medonca were the young guns in the team (born in 2006) and brought so much enthusiasm and excitement into the mix.

title:
Viewed Magnus Carlsen beat D.Gukesh in their first ever classical game after the 18-year-old became the World Champion! 

This was an encounter that a lot of people were waiting for and as a chess fan, I must say it lived upto its potential. Why? Because of Magnus and Gukesh wanted to win! And when both players want to win, it is always a treat for the fans!

What about Carlsen's sedate opening choice? Well, that stemmed more from the fact that he knew how well Gukesh would be prepared in general as he worked with a huge team for the World Championship Match with Ding.

So all that Magnus wanted was a playable position out of the opening. And then the fireworks began! Magnus attacked, Gukesh defended! The real difference was seen when the clock went down below a minute and Gukesh blundered in an equal position to instantly lose the game! Magnus as is always the case, kept his nerves and made it across the finish line!

What does this game mean in the larger scheme of things? Well, the fact that Magnus is the strongest player on the planet is something that Gukesh himself attests to. The question is whether Gukesh will be the one who will be able to dethrone Magnus as the World no.1 in the days to come - something that players like Fabi and Naka have been unsuccessful for well over a decade!

One does feel that the gap between Magnus and the youngsters is reducing! But this has been the case for quite some time now! As of now the Magnus Magic is still strong and he is the best player on the planet! 

Photos: Aditya Sur Roy

#chess #chessbaseindia #norwaychess #magnuscarlsen #gukesh

Viewed If you want a Data Job in the next 3 months:

STOP learning Excel shortcuts, SQL window functions, Python libraries, or Tableau dashboards.

START learning how business works.

- Know why something happens (why users churn, why revenue dips in Month 2);
- Know how to calculate it (profit = revenue – cost, churn = (lost ÷ starting users), stickiness = DAU ÷ MAU);
- Then pick any tool you like—Excel, SQL, Python, Power BI—and implement the same logic everywhere.

When you know the business question and the math behind it, the rest is just implementation.

title:
Viewed Imagine this: 

You're sitting in business class, realizing that all of your hard work and sacrifice have finally paid off.

You have the freedom to decide where and how you want to spend your time and are travelling the world with your family and friends.

This is not just a dream anymore.

You've turned it into reality. Celebrate your wins, be proud of yourself, and don't let anyone talk you out of your own dreams.

It took me 5 years, 1000s of videos and consistent effort to get here.

Comment 🔥 if you agree and are also working on your dreams everyday. Let's grow together.🚀

I spent the last week in San Francisco, attending Google IO, visiting offices of openai, scale ai, lindy ai, and many more, meeting driven founders and most importantly having fun with friends.

I was at Stanford yesterday. In the Graduate School of Business area, there was a quote written on the floor by Phil Knight, the founder of Nike which struck me:

There comes a time in every life when the past recedes and the future opens. It's the moment when you turn to face the unknown. Some will turn back to what they already know. Some will walk straight ahead into uncertainty. I can't tell you which one is right. But I can tell you which one is more fun.

Which path will you choose?

title:
Viewed 🌸 Is this the new best way to integrate custom interactive JS components into Streamlit?

There's a new component "streamlit-anywidget" from mdrazak2001, which makes it easy to add your own bidirectional Jupyter Widgets into a Streamlit app 💪

---

Haven't had time to dig deeper into it but first thoughts I added to my 'to try' list:
- If you can bundle any npm dependencies into a anywidget module, maybe I don't ever need to create a custom Streamlit component again
- Unsure how it interacts with existing ipywidgets libraires, need to dig through

If any of you DataFans test it, don't hesitate to share in the comments below!
video_title:
Viewed 🌸 Is this the new best way to integrate custom interactive JS components into Streamlit?

There's a new component "streamlit-anywidget" from mdrazak2001, which makes it easy to add your own bidirectional Jupyter Widgets into a Streamlit app 💪

---

Haven't had time to dig deeper into it but first thoughts I added to my 'to try' list:
- If you can bundle any npm dependencies into a anywidget module, maybe I don't ever need to create a custom Streamlit component again
- Unsure how it interacts with existing ipywidgets libraires, need to dig through

If any of you DataFans test it, don't hesitate to share in the comments below!

title:
Viewed We’re trying something new on The Diary of a CEO today. 

We’re hosting our first-ever economic debate with two brilliant, but completely opposing minds: Daniel Priestley and Gary Stevenson. 

Daniel is a self-made millionaire and entrepreneur who believes capitalism drives success. Gary, a former Citibank trader turned economist, argues that without wealth redistribution, inequality will break the system.

They disagree on everything and in this conversation, no one holds back.

We cover:
- Why millionaires are leaving the UK…
- How the next recession will be worse than our last?
- What the media and government isn’t telling us about the economy…
- The truth about taxing the rich!
- How the middle class is collapsing?
and so much more!

Two experts, two completely different realities. Who will you agree with?

This debate gets heated, and trust me - You don’t want to miss it!

Let me know your thoughts after 👇

Watch the full episode now by searching "The Diary of a CEO Daniel Priestley Gary Stevenson" on YouTube.

Viewed Grandmaster Vidit Gujrathi books his ticket to Paris! Vidit won the Freestyle Chess Paris Play-in, defeating Richard Rapport 1.5-0.5 in the finals. He has now secured his spot in the Paris Freestyle Chess Grand Slam Tour!

Both the semifinal and final matches took place today. Vidit took down Amin Tabatabaei in the semifinals, winning 2-0! In the finals against Richard Rapport, Vidit once again won a stellar game with the Black pieces in the 1st round, and was winning in game 2 - but a draw was enough to clinch the match. Big congratulations to Vidit Gujrathi - what a dominating performance today!

This will be Vidit Gujrathi's first Freestyle Chess event over-the-board. There are 4 Indian players in the field now - D Gukesh, Arjun Erigaisi, R Praggnanandhaa, and Vidit Gujrathi who will be up against the best in the World - Magnus Carlsen, Fabiano Caruana, Vincent Keymer, Hikaru Nakamura, and more! With a 200,000 USD prize for the Champion, this may very well be the strongest Freestyle Chess tournament so far.

Graphic: Anmol Bhargav
#chess #chessbaseindia #viditgujrathi #freestylechess

title:
Viewed 🎂 Just turned 3️⃣0️⃣ recently 🥳 You could say I'm getting old! 

I had a wonderful trip with my wife in Vietnam -- beautiful country, extremely delicious food and super friendly people. Highly recommend visiting to anyone! Just wanted to share some moments with you, and more importantly I wanted to share this. 

It's okay to not have things figured out. It's okay to struggle. It's okay to not be okay. 

When I was younger, I thought I would have EVERYTHING figured out by the time I'm 30. Well, guess what? I don't. I'm only human and I fight my various battles every single day. 

The big lesson for me in my 20s was learning to enjoy the journey and the process. I try to be more present in the present, not dwell too much on the past and not worry too much about the future. Some days I do a better job, some days I do worse. But I keep on trying every day, that's for sure.
